summary: Markets extended their rally on Friday as the S&P 500 and Nasdaq hit fresh all-time highs, propelled by Apple's 3% surge after the iPhone maker topped Q2 earnings estimates and issued upbeat guidance. Oil prices retreated 2% on reports that Iran sent a peace proposal through Pakistani mediators, easing near-term inflation fears. The Fed's dramatic 8-4 split vote Wednesday to hold rates steady at 3.5-3.75% continues to reverberate, with four dissenting FOMC members—the most since 1992—underscoring deep uncertainty over Middle East war risks and elevated energy-driven inflation. Powell confirmed he will remain as a Fed governor after his May 15 term as chair ends, citing legal pressures. Bitcoin climbed back above $77,000 supported by mild ETF inflows, while Ethereum hovered near $2,300 as outflows persisted. Q1 earnings season is showing 15% year-over-year growth, with 84% of S&P 500 companies beating estimates, though tech faces scrutiny over AI spending returns.

signals.title: Apple earnings beat drives tech rally to new records
signals.desc: Apple reported 17% revenue growth and better-than-expected guidance despite iPhone sales miss, lifting the stock 3-4% and pushing the S&P 500 and Nasdaq to all-time highs.

signals.title: Oil falls on Iran peace proposal reports
signals.desc: WTI crude dropped 2% after reports Iran sent response to U.S. peace amendments via Pakistan mediators, reducing near-term energy shock and inflation tail risk.

signals.title: Historic Fed dissent signals policy paralysis
signals.desc: The FOMC's 8-4 split decision—most dissents since 1992—reveals deep division over easing bias amid Middle East uncertainty and elevated inflation, clouding rate-cut path.

signals.title: Ethereum ETF outflows hit four consecutive days
signals.desc: Ethereum spot ETFs recorded $183.65 million in net outflows through Thursday, signaling fading institutional appetite despite Bitcoin ETF stabilization.

signals.title: Roblox slashes full-year guidance on safety measures
signals.desc: Roblox stock plunged 24% premarket after cutting 2026 bookings forecast by 14-18 percentage points due to mandatory age verification throttling user growth.

verdict: Risk-on momentum remains intact as equity markets look past Fed paralysis and Middle East risks, but fragile peace hopes and stretched valuations warrant caution into next week's ISM and payrolls data.
